_README: Project-scoped MCP config for dream-skill. Loaded via `claude --mcp-config <this-file> --strict-mcp-config` from dream.sh. SINGLE PATTERN: local stdio MCPs via npx, tokens in this file's env blocks. No claude.ai integrations, no remote HTTP. This file is the EXAMPLE — copy to `mcp-config.json` (gitignored) and fill in real values. See docs/MCP-SETUP.md for per-MCP setup walkthroughs.
_security: config/mcp-config.json (the real file, not this example) is gitignored. After creating it, run: chmod 600 config/mcp-config.json. Rotate tokens every 90 days. Revoke at source on any suspected leak.
_placeholders: Anything in ${UPPER_CASE} is a sentinel for the setup wizard or a placeholder to replace by hand. Anything like REPLACE_WITH_X is a value to replace before the MCP will work.

filesystem._what: Tier 1. Lets the LLM write the dream report directly to your vault inbox via MCP write_file, and optionally read individual vault pages on demand. Sandboxed to listed directories with realpath enforcement.
filesystem._recommended_scope: Inbox/dream-reports directory only. Expanding to vault root grants write access to all vault pages — defer until v0.3 ships with rollback log.

notion._what: Tier 2. Read Notion pages and databases during reconciliation. Useful if you keep goals, OKRs, or 'current focus' docs in Notion that your vault references.
notion._token_format: Internal Integration Token starting with 'ntn_' or 'secret_'.
notion._share_pages: After creating the integration, manually share each Notion page/database with it via the page's '...' menu → Connections.

google-calendar._what: Tier 2. Read calendar events during reconciliation. Catches stale assumptions about routines and meeting patterns.
google-calendar._setup_steps: 1) Enable Google Calendar API at https://console.cloud.google.com/ 2) Create OAuth Desktop client 3) Download client_secret JSON 4) Run: npx @cocal/google-calendar-mcp auth (see docs/MCP-SETUP.md for full flow)
google-calendar._token_storage: After running 'auth', the package stores the refresh token at ~/.config/google-calendar-mcp/tokens.json (hardcoded path, don't relocate).

gmail._what: Tier 2. Read email metadata (subjects, senders, dates) during reconciliation. Surfaces life-state signals from comms outside chat history.
gmail._setup_steps: 1) Enable Gmail API in same Google Cloud project as Calendar 2) Create separate OAuth Desktop client 3) Download client_secret JSON 4) Run: GMAIL_OAUTH_PATH=... GMAIL_CREDENTIALS_PATH=... npx -y @gongrzhe/server-gmail-autoauth-mcp auth
gmail._scopes: Read-only is sufficient. Do not grant write/send scopes.
